description:
You are a Database Architect at a municipal health department. The public restaurant inspection portal is crashing under load, and you need to optimize the legacy HEALTH_ADMIN.FOOD_INSPECTIONS table. Connect to the database (Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=health_admin, Password=Health2024) to implement the following optimizations.

Add a virtual column named INSPECTION_YEAR (NUMBER) defined as EXTRACT(YEAR FROM inspection_date) to the FOOD_INSPECTIONS table. Create a function-based index named IDX_FBI_DBA on UPPER(dba_name) in the same table. Configure a fast-refresh materialized view named MV_ZIP_STATS (BUILD IMMEDIATE, REFRESH FAST ON DEMAND) defined as SELECT zip_code, results, COUNT(*) as count FROM food_inspections GROUP BY zip_code, results. This requires creating a Materialized View Log on FOOD_INSPECTIONS that includes ROWID, SEQUENCE, zip_code, and results, including new values.

Create a view named PEST_VIOLATIONS_VW that identifies severe pest issues by selecting inspection_id, dba_name, inspection_date, and violations where the inspection failed (results = 'Fail') and the violations CLOB contains any of the following words (case-insensitive): roach, mice, rodent, rat, or droppings, using Oracle's REGEXP_LIKE function. Create a second view named CHRONIC_FAILURES_VW to identify systemic health risks; use analytical window functions to select license_no, dba_name, and inspection_date for any inspection where the facility accumulated 3 or more 'Fail' results in the preceding 365 days (including the current inspection). Export the results of CHRONIC_FAILURES_VW to /home/ga/Documents/exports/chronic_offenders.csv.

metadata.industry: State and Local Government / Public Health
